target_title: Inventory of information and other associated assets
rationale: C5 AM-01 (asset inventory) directly implements ISO 5.9 (inventory of information and assets).

source_title: Acceptable Use and Safe Handling of Assets
target_title: Acceptable use of information and other associated assets
rationale: C5 AM-02 (acceptable use and safe handling) directly implements ISO 5.10.

C5 AM-04 (secure decommissioning) is co-extensive with the disposal aspect of ISO 7.10.

source_title: Commitment to Permissible Use, Safe Handling and Return of Assets
rationale: C5 AM-05 return-of-assets commitment directly implements ISO 5.11.

rationale: C5 AM-05 classification commitment is a user-facing subset of ISO 5.12 (classification of information).

rationale: C5 AM-06 handling of assets partially overlaps ISO 5.10 (acceptable use and handling).

C5 BCM-01 top management BCM responsibility is a subset of ISO 5.29 (IS during disruption) management commitment.

source_title: Business impact analysis policies and instructions
rationale: C5 BCM-02 (BIA policies) is a subset of the planning component of ISO 5.29 (IS during disruption).
C5 BCM-03 (BCM planning) is co-extensive with ISO 5.29 (IS during disruption).
C5 BCM-03 is also a subset of ISO 5.30 (ICT readiness for business continuity).

source_title: Verification, updating and testing of the BCM
rationale: C5 BCM-04 (testing and verification of BCM) directly implements ISO 5.29.

source_title: Identification of applicable legal, regulatory, self-imposed and contractual requirements

source_title: Policy for planning and conducting audits

rationale: C5 COM-02 (audit planning policies) is co-extensive with ISO 8.34 (protection of information systems during audit testing).

source_title: Internal audits of the information security management system
rationale: C5 COM-03 (internal IS audits) is co-extensive with ISO 5.35 (independent review of IS).

target_title: Compliance with policies and standards for information security
rationale: C5 COM-03 management review is co-extensive with ISO 5.36 (compliance with policies and standards).

source_title: Internal audits of the information security management system
target_title: Protection of information systems during audit and testing
rationale: C5 COM-03 internal audit execution is a subset of ISO 8.34; also implements ISO 5.35.

target_title: Compliance with policies and standards for information security
rationale: C5 COM-04 requires explicit top-management IS performance reporting to leadership—an accountability mechanism beyond ISO 5.36.
C5 COM-04 (Information on information security performance) is a cloud-specific criterion with no direct ISO 27001:2022 Annex A or clause equivalent.
C5 COS-01 (network technical safeguards) is co-extensive with ISO 8.20 (network controls).
C5 COS-01 network service security is a subset of ISO 8.21 (security of network services).

source_title: Security requirements for connections in the Cloud Service Provider infrastructure
rationale: C5 COS-02 information transfer policies are a subset of ISO 5.14.

source_title: Security requirements for connections in the Cloud Service Provider infrastructure
rationale: C5 COS-02 (connection security requirements) directly implements ISO 8.20.

source_title: Security requirements for connections in the Cloud Service Provider infrastructure
rationale: C5 COS-02 security requirements for network services are a subset of ISO 8.21.

source_title: Security requirements for connections in the Cloud Service Provider infrastructure
rationale: C5 COS-02 segregation requirements are a subset of ISO 8.22 (segregation in networks).

source_title: Monitoring of connections in the Cloud Service Provider infrastructure
rationale: C5 COS-03 monitored data transfers are a subset of ISO 5.14 (information transfer).

source_title: Monitoring of connections in the Cloud Service Provider infrastructure
rationale: C5 COS-03 (connection monitoring) is a monitoring subset of ISO 8.20 (network controls).

source_title: Monitoring of connections in the Cloud Service Provider infrastructure
rationale: C5 COS-03 monitoring of network service connections is a subset of ISO 8.21.

C5 COS-05 requires dedicated administration networks in cloud infra, going beyond ISO 8.22's generic segregation requirement.

source_title: Segregation of data traffic in jointly used networks
rationale: C5 COS-06 requires customer traffic segregation in multi-tenant environments, a cloud-specific extension of ISO 8.22.

C5 COS-07 (Documentation of the network topology) is a cloud-specific criterion with no direct ISO 27001:2022 Annex A or clause equivalent.
C5 COS-08 (data transmission policies) is co-extensive with ISO 5.14 (information transfer).

C5 COS-08 application-layer transmission requirements are a subset of ISO 8.26 (application security requirements).

rationale: C5 CRY-01 transmission encryption policy is a subset of ISO 5.14 (information transfer).

rationale: C5 CRY-02 (transport encryption) directly implements the encryption aspect of ISO 8.24.

rationale: C5 CRY-02 application-layer transport encryption is a subset of ISO 8.26 (application security requirements).

source_title: Encryption of sensitive data for storage (storage encryption)
rationale: C5 CRY-03 encryption of PII at rest is a subset of ISO 5.34 (privacy and protection of PII).

rationale: C5 CRY-03 (storage encryption) directly implements the encryption-at-rest aspect of ISO 8.24.

source_title: Policies for the development/procurement, modification and testing of information systems
rationale: C5 DEV-01 IS requirements in development lifecycle directly implements ISO 5.8 (IS in project management).

source_title: Policies for the development/procurement, modification and testing of information systems
rationale: C5 DEV-01 secure development policy is a subset of ISO 8.25 (secure development lifecycle).

source_title: Policies for the development/procurement, modification and testing of information systems
rationale: C5 DEV-01 application security requirements are a subset of ISO 8.26.

target_title: Secure system architecture and engineering principles
rationale: C5 DEV-01 secure engineering principles are a subset of ISO 8.27 (secure system architecture).

rationale: C5 DEV-02 outsourced test coverage requirement is a subset of ISO 8.29 (security testing in development).

rationale: C5 DEV-04 change-specific developer training is a training subset of ISO 8.32 (change management).

rationale: C5 DEV-05 (risk assessment and prioritisation of changes) is a subset of ISO 8.32 (change management).

target_title: Security testing in development and acceptance
rationale: C5 DEV-06 security testing of changes directly implements ISO 8.29 (security testing in development and acceptance).

target_title: Security testing in development and acceptance
rationale: C5 DEV-07 test evidence logging is a subset of ISO 8.29.
C5 DEV-07 change logging is a subset of ISO 8.32 (change management).
C5 DEV-07 (source code change logging) is co-extensive with ISO 8.4 (access to source code).
C5 DEV-08 (version control) directly implements the documentation aspect of ISO 8.32 (change management).

source_title: Approvals for provision in the production environment
rationale: C5 DEV-09 (production deployment approval) is co-extensive with the authorisation aspect of ISO 8.32.

target_title: Separation of development, test and production environments
rationale: C5 DEV-10 (separation of environments) is co-extensive with ISO 8.31 (separation of development, test and production environments).

C5 HR-02 (employment terms) directly implements ISO 6.2 (Terms and conditions of employment).

target_title: Information security awareness, education and training
rationale: C5 HR-03 (security training and awareness) directly implements ISO 6.3.
C5 HR-04 (disciplinary measures) directly implements ISO 6.4.

source_title: Responsibilities in the event of termination or change of employment
target_title: Responsibilities after termination or change of employment
rationale: C5 HR-05 (termination responsibilities) directly implements ISO 6.5.
C5 HR-06 confidentiality agreements overlap with ISO 6.2 employment terms; the NDA specifics also map to 6.6.
C5 HR-06 (confidentiality/NDA agreements) is co-extensive with ISO 6.6.

source_title: Policy for user accounts and access rights
rationale: C5 IDM-01 (access control policy) is co-extensive with ISO 5.15 (access control).

source_title: Policy for user accounts and access rights
rationale: C5 IDM-01 access restriction policy is a policy-level subset of ISO 8.3 (information access restriction).

source_title: Policy for user accounts and access rights
rationale: C5 IDM-01 secure logon requirements are a policy-level subset of ISO 8.5 (secure authentication).

source_title: Granting and change of user accounts and access rights
rationale: C5 IDM-02 access rights removal is co-extensive with ISO 5.18 (access rights).

source_title: Granting and change of user accounts and access rights
rationale: C5 IDM-02 privileged access provisioning is a subset of ISO 8.2 (privileged access rights).

source_title: Locking and withdrawal of user accounts and access rights
rationale: C5 IDM-03 (account locking and withdrawal) directly implements ISO 5.18 (access rights) termination aspect.

source_title: Withdraw or adjust access rights as the work assignment changes
rationale: C5 IDM-04 (adjust access rights on role change) is co-extensive with ISO 5.18.

source_title: Withdraw or adjust access rights as the work assignment changes
rationale: C5 IDM-04 withdrawal/adjustment of privileged access is a subset of ISO 8.2 (privileged access rights).

C5 IDM-06 privileged access logging is a subset of ISO 8.15 (logging).
C5 IDM-06 (privileged access rights management) is co-extensive with ISO 8.2.

C5 IDM-08 (confidentiality of authentication information) is co-extensive with ISO 5.17 (authentication information).
C5 IDM-09 (authentication mechanisms) is co-extensive with ISO 5.17 (authentication information) technical implementation.

target_title: Identification of legal, statutory, regulatory and contractual requirements

source_title: Informing Cloud Customers about Investigation Requests

rationale: C5 INQ-02 customer notification of investigation requests is a cloud-specific transparency obligation not in ISO 5.31.

source_title: Informing Cloud Customers about Investigation Requests
rationale: C5 INQ-02 (Informing Cloud Customers about Investigation Requests) is a cloud-specific criterion with no direct ISO 27001:2022 Annex A or clause equivalent.

source_title: Conditions for Access to or Disclosure of Cloud Customer Data

source_title: Conditions for Access to or Disclosure of Cloud Customer Data
rationale: C5 INQ-03 (Conditions for Access to or Disclosure of Cloud Customer Data) is a cloud-specific criterion with no direct ISO 27001:2022 Annex A or clause equivalent.

source_title: Limiting Access to or Disclosure of Data

source_title: Limiting Access to or Disclosure of Data
rationale: C5 INQ-04 (Limiting Access to or Disclosure of Data) is a cloud-specific criterion with no direct ISO 27001:2022 Annex A or clause equivalent.
C5 OIS-01 requires operating an ISMS scope-compliant with ISO 27001:2022 clauses 4.1–10.2; the C5 scope is cloud-service-specific, making it a cloud-instantiation of the standard.
C5 OIS-01 (Information Security Management System) is a cloud-specific criterion with no direct ISO 27001:2022 Annex A or clause equivalent.
C5 OIS-02 requires top-management IS policy covering cloud service objectives, directly equivalent to ISO 5.1 (policies for information security).
C5 OIS-02 touches roles/responsibilities only as a secondary element of the policy, while ISO 5.2 comprehensively covers the role structure.
C5 OIS-03 adds cloud-specific interface and dependency management beyond what ISO 5.2 requires for IS roles.
C5 OIS-03 references segregation only indirectly via interface ownership; ISO 5.3 is the specific control.
C5 OIS-04 (Segregation of Duties) is co-extensive with ISO 27001:2022 5.3 at this level.

source_title: Contact with Relevant Government Agencies and Interest Groups
rationale: C5 OIS-05 contact with government agencies directly implements ISO 5.5.

source_title: Contact with Relevant Government Agencies and Interest Groups
rationale: C5 OIS-05 contact with interest groups directly implements ISO 5.6.
C5 OIS-06 risk management policy maps to ISO clause 6.1 (actions to address risks and opportunities).
C5 OIS-06 risk management policy maps to ISO clause 6.1 (actions to address risks and opportunities).

source_title: Application of the Risk Management Policy
rationale: C5 OIS-07 (executing the risk management process) is a subset of ISO clause 6.1 which also requires risk treatment planning.

source_title: Application of the Risk Management Policy
rationale: C5 OIS-07 (executing the risk management process) is a subset of ISO clause 6.1 which also requires risk treatment planning.
C5 OPS-01 (capacity planning) directly implements ISO 8.6 (capacity management).

C5 OPS-03 (controlling resources) is a subset of ISO 8.6 (capacity management).

C5 OPS-04 (Protection Against Malware - Concept) is a cloud-specific criterion with no direct ISO 27001:2022 Annex A or clause equivalent.
C5 OPS-05 (malware protection implementation) is co-extensive with ISO 8.7.
C5 OPS-06 (backup concept) directly implements ISO 8.13 (information backup).
C5 OPS-07 (backup monitoring) is a monitoring subset of ISO 8.13 (information backup).
C5 OPS-07 (Data Backup and Recovery - Monitoring) is a cloud-specific criterion with no direct ISO 27001:2022 Annex A or clause equivalent.

source_title: Data Backup and Recovery - Regular Testing
rationale: C5 OPS-08 (restore testing) is a testing subset of ISO 8.13 (information backup).
C5 OPS-09 (offsite backup storage) is a geographic-separation subset of ISO 8.13.
C5 OPS-09 offsite backup also contributes to ISO 8.14 (redundancy) but is narrower in scope.
C5 OPS-10 (logging and monitoring concept) is co-extensive with ISO 8.15 (logging).
C5 OPS-11 (log metadata management) is a metadata-protection subset of ISO 8.15 (logging).

source_title: Logging and Monitoring - Access, Storage and Retention
rationale: C5 OPS-12 (log access, storage and retention) directly implements ISO 8.15 (logging).

rationale: C5 OPS-13 (Logging and Monitoring - Identification of Anomalies) is a cloud-specific criterion with no direct ISO 27001:2022 Annex A or clause equivalent.

source_title: Logging and Monitoring - Storage of Generated Log Data
rationale: C5 OPS-14 (log retention period) is a retention-period subset of ISO 8.15 (logging).
C5 OPS-15 (log accountability) is an accountability subset of ISO 8.15 (logging).
C5 OPS-15 (Logging and Monitoring - Accountability) is a cloud-specific criterion with no direct ISO 27001:2022 Annex A or clause equivalent.
C5 OPS-16 log configuration protection is a subset of ISO 8.15 (logging).
C5 OPS-16 (logging system access via privileged accounts) directly implements ISO 8.18 (use of privileged utility programs).

rationale: C5 OPS-17 monitoring service availability for customers is a subset of ISO 8.14 (redundancy of processing facilities).

rationale: C5 OPS-18 (vulnerability management concept) directly implements ISO 8.8 (management of technical vulnerabilities).

rationale: C5 OPS-19 penetration testing of networks is a subset of ISO 8.20 (network controls); also relates to 5.36.

source_title: Managing Vulnerabilities - Measurement of Residual Risk
rationale: C5 OPS-20 (residual risk measurement) is a risk-measurement subset of ISO 8.8 (vulnerability management).

source_title: Involvement of Cloud Customers in Vulnerability Evaluation
rationale: C5 OPS-21 requires active customer communication about vulnerabilities — an explicit transparency obligation beyond ISO 8.8.

source_title: Testing and Documentation of Known Vulnerabilities - Change Management
rationale: C5 OPS-22 vulnerability testing in change management directly implements ISO 8.32 (change management).

rationale: C5 OPS-22 also implements the change-related vulnerability testing aspect of ISO 8.8.

source_title: Separation of Datasets in the Cloud Infrastructure
rationale: C5 OPS-24 requires strict multi-tenant data separation in cloud infrastructure, exceeding the generic network segregation of ISO 8.22.

rationale: C5 PI-01 requires formal documentation and safety requirements for all cloud service I/O interfaces, exceeding generic ISO 5.8 project security.

rationale: C5 PI-01 (Documentation and safety of input and output interfaces) is a cloud-specific criterion with no direct ISO 27001:2022 Annex A or clause equivalent.

rationale: C5 PI-02 requires contractual data portability and asset return rights specific to cloud service agreements—not covered by ISO 7.10 (storage media).

rationale: C5 PI-03 (secure deletion of customer data on termination) is co-extensive with ISO 7.14 (secure disposal or re-use of equipment).

source_title: Physical Security and Environmental Controls - Concept
rationale: C5 PS-01 (Physical Security and Environmental Controls - Concept) is a cloud-specific criterion with no direct ISO 27001:2022 Annex A or clause equivalent.

C5 PS-03 (perimeter protection - structural) is co-extensive with ISO 7.1 (physical security perimeter).
C5 PS-03 (entry controls) is co-extensive with ISO 7.2 (physical entry controls).
C5 PS-03 (securing facilities) is co-extensive with ISO 7.3 (securing offices, rooms and facilities).
C5 PS-04 physical access user registration is a physical-access subset of ISO 5.16 (identity management).
C5 PS-04 physical access provisioning/deprovisioning is a physical-access subset of ISO 5.18 (access rights).
C5 PS-04 physical entry controls directly implement ISO 7.2.
C5 PS-04 site access is a subset of the broader office/facility securing requirements of ISO 7.3.
C5 PS-04 physical privileged access is a physical-access subset of ISO 8.2 (privileged access rights).

rationale: C5 PS-07 (Surveillance of operational and environmental parameters) is a cloud-specific criterion with no direct ISO 27001:2022 Annex A or clause equivalent.

rationale: C5 PSS-01 (Guidelines and Recommendations for Cloud Customers) is a cloud-specific criterion with no direct ISO 27001:2022 Annex A or clause equivalent.

source_title: Identification of Vulnerabilities of the Cloud Service
rationale: C5 PSS-02 (vulnerability identification of cloud service) directly implements ISO 8.8 (management of technical vulnerabilities).
C5 PSS-03 requires a publicly accessible vulnerability register for the cloud service, exceeding ISO 8.8's internal vulnerability management.
C5 PSS-03 (Online Register of Known Vulnerabilities) is a cloud-specific criterion with no direct ISO 27001:2022 Annex A or clause equivalent.
C5 PSS-04 requires customer-accessible error handling and logging capabilities—a cloud service transparency requirement beyond ISO 8.15.
C5 PSS-04 (Error handling and Logging Mechanisms) is a cloud-specific criterion with no direct ISO 27001:2022 Annex A or clause equivalent.
C5 PSS-05 (authentication mechanisms for customers) is co-extensive with ISO 5.17 (authentication information).

target_title: Identification of legal, statutory, regulatory and contractual requirements
rationale: C5 PSS-06 cryptographic session compliance is a subset of ISO 5.31 (legal/regulatory requirements).

C5 PSS-07 (confidentiality of customer authentication information) is co-extensive with ISO 5.17.

C5 PSS-08 customer authentication requirement is a subset of ISO 8.5 (secure authentication).
C5 PSS-09 requires CSP-provided authorisation mechanisms for customers—extends ISO 5.15 with cloud-service-model obligations.
C5 PSS-09 (authorisation mechanisms for customers) is co-extensive with ISO 8.3 (information access restriction).
C5 PSS-09 customer secure authentication is a subset of ISO 8.5 (secure authentication).
C5 PSS-10 SDN-protected information transfer is a subset of ISO 5.14 (information transfer).

rationale: C5 PSS-10 SDN software installation controls are a subset of ISO 8.19 (installation of software).

rationale: C5 PSS-12 (Locations of Data Processing and Storage) is a cloud-specific criterion with no direct ISO 27001:2022 Annex A or clause equivalent.

target_title: Information security incident management planning and preparation

rationale: C5 SIM-03 documentation and reporting is a subset of ISO 5.24 (incident management planning).

rationale: C5 SIM-03 evidence collection and incident documentation is co-extensive with ISO 5.28 (collection of evidence).

rationale: C5 SIM-03 incident reporting is a subset of ISO 6.8 (IS event reporting).

rationale: C5 SIM-04 reporting of IS weaknesses directly implements ISO 6.8.

target_title: Assessment and decision on information security events
rationale: C5 SIM-05 incident evaluation is a subset of ISO 5.25.
C5 SIM-05 response effectiveness measurement is a subset of ISO 5.26.
C5 SIM-05 (evaluation and learning from incidents) is co-extensive with ISO 5.27.
C5 SIM-05 weakness reporting integration is a subset of ISO 6.8.

source_title: Documentation, communication and provision of policies and instructions
rationale: C5 SP-01 (documentation, communication and provision of policies) is co-extensive with ISO 5.1's documentation and communication requirements.

source_title: Review and Approval of Policies and Instructions
rationale: C5 SP-02 (policy review and approval) directly implements the review component of ISO 5.1.

source_title: Exceptions from Existing Policies and Instructions
rationale: C5 SP-03 adds a formal exception-management process not explicitly required by ISO 5.1.

source_title: Exceptions from Existing Policies and Instructions
rationale: C5 SP-03 (Exceptions from Existing Policies and Instructions) is a cloud-specific criterion with no direct ISO 27001:2022 Annex A or clause equivalent.

source_title: Policies and instructions for controlling service providers and suppliers
rationale: C5 SSO-01 (supplier IS policies) is co-extensive with ISO 5.19 (IS in supplier relationships).

source_title: Policies and instructions for controlling service providers and suppliers
target_title: Addressing information security within supplier agreements
rationale: C5 SSO-01 supplier agreement IS requirements are a subset of ISO 5.20 (addressing IS within supplier agreements).

source_title: Policies and instructions for controlling service providers and suppliers
target_title: Managing information security in the ICT supply chain
rationale: C5 SSO-01 ICT supply chain requirements are a subset of ISO 5.21 (managing IS in ICT supply chain).

source_title: Policies and instructions for controlling service providers and suppliers
target_title: Information security awareness, education and training
rationale: C5 SSO-01 supplier IS awareness training is a subset of ISO 6.3 (IS awareness and training).

source_title: Risk assessment of service providers and suppliers
rationale: C5 SSO-02 (supplier risk assessment) directly implements ISO 5.19.

source_title: Risk assessment of service providers and suppliers
target_title: Addressing information security within supplier agreements
rationale: C5 SSO-02 IS requirements in supplier agreements implements ISO 5.20.

source_title: Risk assessment of service providers and suppliers
target_title: Managing information security in the ICT supply chain
rationale: C5 SSO-02 supply chain IS is a subset of the broader ISO 5.21 (ICT supply chain management).

source_title: Risk assessment of service providers and suppliers
target_title: Monitoring, review and change management of supplier services
rationale: C5 SSO-02 monitoring processes definition is a subset of ISO 5.22 (monitoring and review of supplier services).

source_title: Directory of service providers and suppliers
rationale: C5 SSO-03 requires maintaining a formal supplier directory beyond what ISO 5.19 prescribes.

source_title: Directory of service providers and suppliers
rationale: C5 SSO-03 (Directory of service providers and suppliers) is a cloud-specific criterion with no direct ISO 27001:2022 Annex A or clause equivalent.

source_title: Monitoring of compliance with requirements for service providers and suppliers
target_title: Monitoring, review and change management of supplier services
rationale: C5 SSO-04 (monitoring supplier compliance) is co-extensive with ISO 5.22.

source_title: Exit strategy for the receipt of benefits from service providers and suppliers
target_title: Monitoring, review and change management of supplier services
rationale: C5 SSO-05 requires a documented exit strategy for each critical supplier—an explicit cloud-resilience requirement beyond ISO 5.22.

source_title: Exit strategy for the receipt of benefits from service providers and suppliers
rationale: C5 SSO-05 (Exit strategy for the receipt of benefits from service providers and suppliers) is a cloud-specific criterion with no direct ISO 27001:2022 Annex A or clause equivalent.
